stimulus: The Inheritors, a 1901 novel jointly written by Joseph Conrad and Ford Madox Ford, stands among the rare instances of ______blank in literature. Most authors prefer to compose alone, and given that the difficulties of the joint effort strained the partnership between Conrad and Ford, it is easy to see why.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical and precise word or phrase?
rationale: Choice D is the best answer because "co-authorship" precisely names two writers composing a single work together, which is the unusual practice the text highlights. Choice A is incorrect because "experimentation" refers to trying novel techniques; the text emphasizes shared authorship, not novelty of method. Choice B is incorrect because "translation" refers to rendering text from one language to another and does not describe joint composition. Choice C is incorrect because "satire" describes a tone or genre and does not address the collaborative nature the text discusses.
